hooks.command: bash -c 'if [ -f memory/goals.md ]; then echo "=== PROJECT GOALS ==="; cat memory/goals.md; fi; LATEST=$(ls -t memory/episodic/*.md 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [ -n "$LATEST" ]; then echo "\n=== LATEST SESSION ==="; cat "$LATEST"; fi'

bash -c 'if [ -f memory/working_snapshot.md ]; then echo "=== WORKING SNAPSHOT (pre-compact) ==="; cat memory/working_snapshot.md; rm memory/working_snapshot.md; fi; if [ -f CONTEXT.md ]; then echo "\n=== CONTEXT ==="; cat CONTEXT.md; fi; if [ -f memory/goals.md ]; then echo "\n=== GOALS ==="; cat memory/goals.md; fi'

hooks.prompt:
Before compaction, do two things:

1. Write memory/working_snapshot.md with this structure:
```
# Working Snapshot
## Active Task
[task ID, status, what phase]
## Current Activity
[what you were doing right now, specific enough to resume]
## Decisions Made This Session
- [decision]: [rationale]
## Blocked Tasks
[any tasks parked with reason, or "None"]
## In-Flight State
[anything that would be lost — partial analysis, uncommitted findings, etc.]
```
Keep it under 30 lines.

2. Check if any new project facts or validated patterns emerged this session that haven't been written to memory yet. If so, write them now:
- New facts → memory/semantic/ (create file if needed)
- Validated patterns or failures → memory/procedural/ (create file if needed)
Skip this if nothing new emerged. Don't force it.

Check if significant work was done this session — this includes code changes, decisions, research findings, design work, discovery sessions, or any task progress. Non-commit sessions (research, design, discovery) still produce state worth saving. If significant work was done, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "Consider running /context-save before ending the session."}. If the session was just Q&A or trivial, respond with {"ok": true}.
